mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so sustained cuts predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative during periods of month-over-month FEDFUNDS decline, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: An upward break in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and weaker future household income, which should pressure risk appetite and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign is not negative relative to the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad real-activity acceleration and stronger final demand, which should lift corporate revenue expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: both GDPC1 and RSAFS are above their prior 4-quarter and 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if periods with both proxies above trend do not show higher 60-day SPX returns than periods with only one or neither above trend, or if the joint signal has no incremental explanatory power.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO reflects improving industrial throughput and inventory stabilization, which should confirm cyclical growth momentum and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month increases in INDPRO do not correspond to higher-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns over walk-forward tests, or if the sign flips in more than one 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals housing weakness and a future slowdown in consumer demand, so when housing starts fall below a multi-month trend the S&P 500 should underperform over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend HOUST observations are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the signal is indistinguishable from noise after revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains near the lower bound, real rates stay depressed and policy stays supportive, which should be bullish for S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL trend is positive and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the combination of rising CPIAUCSL and sub-1% FEDFUNDS does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more frequently than the unconditional sample, or if higher inflation with low rates instead correlates with negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS indicates payroll contraction and weaker earnings power, so a falling payroll trend should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter in an ongoing slowdown.
failure_condition: Reject if negative PAYEMS momentum is not associated with negative 60-day SPX returns across rolling samples, or if the relationship vanishes after controlling for UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A demand rebound requires both stronger retail sales and improving housing activity; when RSAFS rises together with HOUST, equity returns should be positive over the next month because the signal captures broad household demand recovery.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST rise versus their prior month
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly increases in RSAFS and HOUST do not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered cases are not net positive over walk-forward tests.
